description_long: This object is a portable metal crowd control barrier, commonly seen in urban environments for managing pedestrian or vehicle flow. It features a rectangular frame with rounded corners and six evenly spaced vertical bars, providing a sturdy yet open structure. The barrier stands on two pairs of angled support legs that extend outward for stability, and each end of the frame has a small protruding tab, likely for interlocking with adjacent barriers. The construction appears to be tubular steel, giving it both strength and moderate weight. The surface is uniformly coated in a dark, likely powder-coated finish, offering weather resistance and durability. There are no visible reflective markings or additional attachments.
description: A portable metal crowd control barrier with a rectangular frame, six vertical bars, and angled support legs, finished in a dark color.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular frame, six vertical bars, and one end tab on the left.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the barrier and the outward-angled support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the end tab now on the right side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and support legs.
